relational-model-basics.label: Relational Model Basics
relational-model-basics.summary: Understand tables, rows, and columns in relational databases.

sketch-relational-tables.title: What is a Table?
sketch-relational-tables.bodyMarkdown: In a relational database, data is organized into tables. Each table represents a collection of related information, similar to a spreadsheet. Tables are made up of rows and columns.

sketch-rows-columns.title: Rows and Columns
sketch-rows-columns.bodyMarkdown: Each row in a table represents a single record, while each column represents a specific attribute of that record. For example, in a table of students, each row is a student, and columns might include name, age, and grade.

sketch-relations.title: Relations in Databases
sketch-relations.bodyMarkdown: A 'relation' is another word for a table in the relational model. The structure of a table (its columns) is called its schema. The data in the table (the rows) are called tuples.

quiz-single-choice-table-definition.title: What best describes a table in a relational database?
quiz-single-choice-table-definition.prompt: Choose the best definition of a table in the context of relational databases.
quiz-single-choice-table-definition.hint: Think about how data is organized in a spreadsheet.

help.concept: A table in a relational database organizes data into rows and columns, where each row is a record and each column is an attribute.
help.hint_1: Tables are like spreadsheets: they have rows and columns.
help.hint_2: A table groups related data together, not just a single value or a list of commands.

options.a: A collection of related data organized in rows and columns.
options.b: A list of commands for the database.
options.c: A single value stored in the database.

quiz-drag-reorder-table-structure.title: Order the components of a table
quiz-drag-reorder-table-structure.prompt: Arrange the following from the broadest to the most specific in a table.
quiz-drag-reorder-table-structure.hint: Start with the largest structure and end with the smallest data unit.

help.concept: A table contains columns and rows; each row is made up of cells, which are the intersection of a row and a column.
help.hint_1: Think about how a spreadsheet is structured.
help.hint_2: A cell is the smallest piece, while the table is the largest.

quiz-fill-blank-choice-row-definition.title: What is a row?
quiz-fill-blank-choice-row-definition.prompt: A row in a table represents a single ____.
quiz-fill-blank-choice-row-definition.hint: Rows are individual entries in a table.

help.concept: Each row in a table corresponds to one the missing term or instance of the entity described by the table.
help.hint_1: Rows are not columns or attributes.
help.hint_2: A row is a single entry, like one student in a students table.

quiz-fill-blank-choice-row-definition.template: A row in a table represents a single [[blank]].

quiz-multi-choice-columns-attributes.title: Which of the following describe columns in a table?
quiz-multi-choice-columns-attributes.prompt: Select all statements that correctly describe columns in a relational database table.
quiz-multi-choice-columns-attributes.hint: Columns are about the properties or fields of the data.

help.concept: Columns in a table define the attributes or fields for each record, have unique names, and store values of a specific type.
help.hint_1: Columns are not the same as records.
help.hint_2: Columns describe what kind of data is stored, like 'name' or 'age'.

options.a: They define the attributes of the data.
options.b: Each column has a unique name within the table.
options.d: Columns store values of a specific data type.

quiz-code-input-simple-select.title: Select all rows from a table
quiz-code-input-simple-select.prompt: Write an SQL query to select all columns and rows from the 'students' table.
quiz-code-input-simple-select.hint: Use the SELECT statement to get all data from the table.

help.concept: The SELECT statement retrieves data from a table. Using * selects all columns.
help.hint_1: Use SELECT and FROM with the table name.
help.hint_2: To get every column, use an asterisk (*) after SELECT.
